title: Add FreeBSD support for 'buildah info'
body:
On non-windows platforms, we can use the uname syscall to get kernel
version information. For uptime, we can use clock_gettime(CLOCK_UPTIME)
on FreeBSD but I don't have a solution for darwin or windows.

Note: the existing code which unconditionally reads from /proc builds
on all platforms but only works on linux since using /proc in this way
is a linux feature.

[NO NEW TESTS NEEDED]

Signed-off-by: Doug Rabson <dfr@rabson.org>

#### What type of PR is this?

/kind cleanup

#### What this PR does / why we need it:

The info sub-command is used by buildah tests so fixing it is a precursor to getting some of those tests working on FreeBSD

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?
```release-note
None
```

comments.body: Looks like Linux is missing the CLOCK_UPTIME timer so I'll reinstate the /proc/uptime code. Messy.
Was linux missing it or Darwin (MAC)?

Both, as it turned out. Apparently CLOCK_UPTIME is a FreeBSD invention.

On Fri, 15 Jul 2022 at 16:54, Daniel J Walsh ***@***.***>
wrote:

> Was linux missing it or Darwin (MAC)?
>
> —
> Reply to this email directly, view it on GitHub
> <https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/4114#issuecomment-1185676496>,

> You are receiving this because you authored the thread.Message ID:
> ***@***.***>
>
This seems fine, but I have a feeling Podman would like to use this also.  So should we put this into pkg/util or container/common?

Podman does have a similar pattern and I think it makes sense to try to
share the code. Given that podman imports buildah, putting the code in a
package in buildah would be simplest and I can make the corresponding
change in libpod next time vendor/github.com/containers/buildah is
refreshed.

Yes I think the existence of those is confusing also.
